english: play, game, dgá-bai rtséd-mo byéd-pa (dga' ba'i rtsed mo byed pa) Dzl.; glu gar rtséd-mo byéd-pa (glu gar rtsed mo byed pa) to sing, dance and play Glr.; rtséd-mo (rtsed mo) toy, byís-pai (byis pa'i) children's toy Mil.; rtséd-mo-čan (rtsed mo can) playful, sportive, merry Cs.; rkyál- (rkyal-), gár- (gar-), gri- (gri-), čól- (chol-), mčóṅ- (mchong-), rtá-rtsed (rta rtsed) the sport of swimming, dancing, fencing, dicing, leaping, riding Cs.; ɣyen-rtséd (g-yen rtsed) play, amusement, diversion; rtsed-dgá (rtsed dga') id. Sch.; to-to-liṅ-liṅ rtséd (to to ling ling rtsed) q. v.; rtséd-jo (rtsed jo), rtsén-jo (rtsen jo), W. *sén-jo* (*sen jo*) sport, public amusement, popular pleasure; ɣzón-nu rtséd-joi tsógs-kyis bskór-nas (gzhon nu rtsed jo'i tshogs kyis bskor nas) surrounded by a number of youthful playmates; *sén-jo táṅ-če* (*sen jo tang che*) W. to arrange a sport. II. to varnish (?).
the disagreeable feeling in the teeth produced by acids Sch.; rsed-ám (rsed am) a shivering, cold shudder Sch. v. rtsé-ba (rtse ba) 3.
Mil., acc. to Sch.: calf of the leg.
n. of a certain era or period of the world v. dus (dus) 6.

I. vb., pf. brtsad (brtsad) to contend, to fight with arms Dzl.; with words: to dispute, debate, wrangle, frq., daṅ (dang) with, la (la) about; rtsód-ciṅ mi-snyán rjód-pa (rtsod cing mi snyan rjod pa) to speak evil words, to use bad language, in quarreling. - II. sbst. dispute, contention, quarrel; disputation Glr.; rtsód-pa ˳grán-pa (rtsod pa 'gran pa) to compete in disputation Glr. - tsád-mai rtsód-pa (tshad ma'i rtsod pa) a learned debate about words; rtsód-pa-rnams (rtsod pa rnams) points of controversy Tar. 132,18, Schf. - rtsod-ɣzí (rtsod gzhi) the subject of a disputation.
Pur. nausea, vomiting, *rtson p̌og* (*rtson phog*) he grows-sick; *rtsón-čas* (*rtson chas*) to be sick, to vomit.

I. vb., pf. (b)rtsams ((b)rtsams), rtsoms (rtsoms), fut. brtsam (brtsam), imp. rtsom(s) (rtsom(s)) 1. to begin, commence a work, to be about, to set about an undertaking; ˳brós-par brtsáms-te ('bros par brtsams te) being about to run away Dzl.; čós-las brtsáms-te rtsód-do (chos las brtsams te rtsod do) it was about religion that our dispute began Tar.; ṅo-lóg brtsáms-pa-las (ngo log brtsams pa las) beginning, stirring up an insurrection Glr.; dé-nas brtsáms-te (de nas brtsams te) beginning at this place, from here, from that time (cf. bzúṅs-te (bzungs te) sub bzúṅ-ba (bzung ba)). - 2. to make, to accomplish, ysó-bai las mi brtsám-mo (gso ba'i las mi brtsam mo) so he will not accomplish the business of healing; com. to compose, to draw up, in writing, bstán-bčos rtsom-mi (bstan bcos rtsom mi) author, writer, composer Pth.; brtson-˳grús rtsóm-pa (brtson 'grus rtsom pa) Dzl. frq., to work diligently, carefully; to take pains, to exert one's self, rtsóm-par (rtsom par), or rtsóm-pa-la mkás-pa (rtsom pa la mkhas pa) a clever writer, an elegant composer, which title in Tibet is applied to any one, that exhibits in his style high-sounding bombast with a flourish of religious phrases; čad rtsod rtsom ɣsum-gyi bšad-gra (chad rtsod rtsom gsum gyi bshad gra) Glr. prob. a school, in which religion is taught and explained, combined with disputations and written compositions. - II. sbt. beginning, commencement (आरम्भ), rtsóm-pa daṅ-po (rtsom pa dang po) the first beginning Ld:-Glr.; a doing, proceeding, undertaking, deed Tar.

1. vb. to endeavour, to take pains, to give diligence; rtsól-bar (rtsol bar) adv. diligently, zealously; kyód-kyis rtsól-bai dús-la bab (khyod kyis rtsol ba'i dus la bab) now you must use dispatch Pth.; rtsol-méd (rtsol med) unsought, rtsol-méd ˳gró-bai don byéd-pa (rtsol med 'gro ba'i don byed pa) to seek the welfare of beings without their caring for it Glr.; srog rtsól-ba (srog rtsol ba) Lex. and Mil., acc. to Sch.: to draw breath, to take fresh courage, which seems to be implied by dbugs rtsól-ba (dbugs rtsol ba) Ma.; nyal-po rtsol drag(-na) (nyal po rtsol drag(-na)) if cohabitation is immoderately indulged in Med. - 2. sbst. zeal, endeavour, exertion, rtsól-ba skyéd-pa (rtsol ba skyed pa) to use diligence Zam.

pf. and fut. stsol (stsol) (*sól-wa* (*sol wa*), sál-wa* (sal wa*)), 1. to give, bestow, grant, when the person that gives is respectfully spoken to, much the same as ɣnaṅ-ba (gnang ba) q.v.; stsál-du ɣsol (stsal du gsol) please to give, to grant etc. Dzl.; bdág-gi lám-rgyags stsol čig (bdag gi lam rgyags stsol cig) pray, give me provisions (provender) for the journey Dzl.; to give back, to return what had been lent Dzl.; to grant, bestow, afford, give (as
